title: Sour Velvety Spanish Chwinamul Croquettes
description: A bright, tangy croquette that marries Spanish flavor with Korean chwinamul, made vegan and jain-friendly for easy meal prep.
instructions: Soak the chopped chwinamul in salted water (2 g salt in 200 ml water) for 30 minutes to cure lightly, then drain and rinse.
Boil the peeled potato until tender, then mash into a smooth purée.
In a bowl, combine mashed potato, chickpea flour, oat milk, nutritional yeast, lemon juice, tamari, smoked paprika, sea salt, and black pepper; mix until homogeneous.
Fold the cured chwinamul into the potato mixture until fully incorporated.
Transfer the mixture to a shallow dish and chill in the refrigerator for 30 minutes to firm.
Shape the chilled mixture into small oval patties about 2 cm thick.
Heat the oil in a nonstick skillet over medium heat until a drop of water sizzles.
Fry the croquettes for 3–4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and crisp.
Remove from skillet and drain on paper towels to absorb excess oil.
Serve warm with a squeeze of fresh lemon and a sprinkle of extra smoked paprika for garnish.
